restartStrategy.title: Restart strategy
restartStrategy.desc: Choose how instances are restarted. Each option affects scope and stability.

safe.title: Safe restart (recommended)
safe.principle: Send SIGTERM to the target instance processes so the app can save and exit, then relaunch that instance (force kill if needed).
safe.pros: Closer to a normal exit, less likely to trigger crash prompts, safer for data.
safe.cons: May take longer while waiting for a graceful shutdown.

force.title: Force restart
force.principle: Match processes by --user-data-dir and terminate them forcibly (SIGKILL / taskkill /F), then relaunch the target instance.
force.pros: Can precisely restart a single instance.
force.cons: May trigger “unexpected quit” prompts and risk losing unsaved data.

flowNotice.inject: One-click switching: This tool reads local VS Code encryption material (DPAPI/Keychain/Secret Service), decrypts the auth session in state.vscdb, replaces the GitHub Copilot token, and re-encrypts it — all locally on your machine.
flowNotice.reason: Permission scope: read VS Code auth database (state.vscdb) and call system credential capability (Windows DPAPI / macOS Keychain / Linux Secret Service) for decrypt/write-back.
flowNotice.storage: Data scope: local import reads GitHub auth sessions from VS Code, and switching updates the same entries; OAuth, token import, local-import validation, and quota refresh call GitHub official APIs with required auth fields. state.vscdb and system keys are not uploaded.

flowNotice.title: Kiro account guide (click to expand/collapse)
flowNotice.desc: Account switching reads and writes local Kiro auth files and instance settings on this device; OAuth login and quota refresh call Kiro official APIs. For Enterprise/IdC accounts, refresh also calls AWS IAM Identity Center OIDC APIs.
flowNotice.reason: Processing paths include ~/.aws/sso/cache/kiro-auth-token.json, instance profile.json, and the kiro.kiroAgent entry in state.vscdb.
flowNotice.storage: Processed data includes Kiro session and quota information; requests to Kiro official APIs send required auth fields (such as code, refreshToken, accessToken, and profileArn). Enterprise/IdC refresh additionally sends required OIDC fields (such as client_id, client_secret, and refresh_token).
